text.title: Solo Self Code Review Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Solo Self Code Review Protocol methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every PR MUST be reviewed against a written, version-controlled checklist of >=10 items (tests cover new branches, no print/console.log, error handling on external calls, no hard-coded secrets, migration reversible, feature flag for user-visible changes, etc.); each item ticked or explicitly waived in the PR description.
rule.rationale: Without a written checklist, self-review attention drifts toward whatever felt risky while coding - missing regressions outside that focus.

rule.statement: Before merge, the diff MUST be fed to an AI assistant with a fixed prompt asking for bugs, edge cases missed, security exposures, test gaps; AI response pasted into the PR. Self-review with no AI second-reviewer is rejected.

rationale.source: AI-pair retros; Claude Code blog series

rule.rationale: AI second-review catches a different class of issue than human self-review; treating it as mandatory institutionalises the distance the operator can't get alone.

rule.statement: Repo MUST have a written list of risk-flag path/file patterns (DB migrations, billing, auth, hard deletes, infra config); a PR touching any flagged path is marked RISKY in its title and triggers cool-off.
rule.rationale: Explicit risk flags catch the moment risk-perception fails ('it's just a small migration'); the list survives optimism.

rule.statement: RISKY PRs MUST sit at least 24 hours between 'ready' and merge; the operator MUST re-read the diff at least once with fresh eyes during the cool-off; emergency override requires an open incident or written exception in the PR.
rule.rationale: Solo operators systematically over-trust freshly-written code; mandatory 24h pause produces a fresh re-read that catches obvious bugs.

rule.statement: CI (tests + linter + typecheck) MUST be green at the SHA being merged with no skipped or xit-marked tests; CI failure post-merge on main triggers revert within 30 minutes - fix-forward is forbidden unless the build was green at merge and the failure is environmental.
rule.rationale: Solo merges have no second pair of eyes to catch a yellow CI; revert-first preserves the ability to ship anything else that day.

rule.statement: Checklist + AI response + risk-flag verdict + cool-off timestamps MUST be pasted into the PR body BEFORE merge; absence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without an in-PR record, the methodology cannot be audited; one-off skips become the norm.

rule.statement: The checklist file MUST be versioned in the repo (`.docs/self-review-checklist.md`); changes follow a documented PR cycle with rationale.
rule.rationale: Unversioned checklists drift silently; engineer reorders or removes items without explanation.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
